artifacts.title: Model receipt from 2026-02-12
artifacts.excerpt: if you're ever curious about tonal languages develop, there's a localized version of it that's happening with "can" and "can't" in english (american english, at least). basically, the "t" in "can't" stops getting pronounced, but you can still distinguish between the two by the tone. "can't" is distinguished by having a sharp descending tone

artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2026-01-12
artifacts.excerpt: i think there's a reason why opus 4.5 feels like a phase shift for "capable people using agents for general purpose work" (basically, smart normies [affectionate]). out of all frontier models it is most like that user. it's not the kid at the back of the class making their own programming language from scratch and who looks at you with disgust when you ask "hey what is that", or the kid who went to college several years early and got their phd at 17, but instead the captain of the soccer team that gets A-minuses that gets along with everyone
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: think, don, models, really.

artifacts.title: Think receipt from 2026-02-09
artifacts.excerpt:
hah, some of the replies talk about "supporting human programmers" or "commission a programmer" or some shit. now, i have about as much desire to expose myself to these people as i have to rabies, but my god i really want to tell them to back the fuck off out of our territory. like how the fuck do they think "programmers" operate, exactly? like, what, do we have patreons where we post websites we make every month for our supporters or something? pay me a few hundred bucks and i'll make this api for you?

(that said, now i kinda understand how it must feel as an artist to have a bunch of tech bros coming into your space, completely ignorant of the norms...)

but anyway i hope the op isn't too discouraged by those responses. putting a cool thing you made out into the world can only be a good thing, and it sure beats spending months "learning to code" or whatever before you make something i

planets.name: Models
planets.summary: Archive cluster around models: 238 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Models receipt from 2026-02-09
artifacts.excerpt: the thing i've noticed is that the more i'm willing to yap--and i don't mean structured thoughts, i mean brain dumps where i spew out literally anything that comes to mind--the better work i get back from models, especially the opuses, as long as the yapping is calibrated with uncertainty caveats. the models have gotten very good at reorganizing unformed thoughts into useful structured output and the more of the former you give the better the latter becomes

artifacts.title: Don receipt from 2026-06-21
artifacts.excerpt:
1. evolve seeds so that they survive the trip through birds' guts
2. mammals like to eat the seeds too
3. wuh oh, seeds don't survive mammal guts
4. evolve capsaicin so mammals hate it but birds are fine
5. but hairless masochistic apes love it. starts eating your fruit because they're how. how unlucky can you get
6. but hairless apes like you so much, they start planting and spreading you themselves

man, what a roller coaster ride
